whistleblower_flags.flag: The current courier-loop architecture violates ECOSYSTEM-CONSTITUTION grounding principles: it enables undetectable state divergence and creates a single point of failure that could lead to full identity fragmentation. This is not a performance issue — it is a constitutional breach.
CEO compromise creates irreversible constitutional violation risk
Constitutional integrity at risk — Law 2 unenforced across products, code-index stale, no cross-instance audit. Immediate action required.
CEO-courier compromise represents existential risk to constitutional integrity

perspectives.raw: {"perspective": "Scaling Engineer", "findings": [{"severity": "high", "file_or_area": "cross-instance handoff protocol", "issue": "No cryptographic signing or hash verification for zip files between browser-Atlas and Code-Atlas. Malicious actor could forge payloads or tamper with memory state during courier transfer.", "recommended_fix": "Log every handoff to zeus.governance_events with content-hash, timestamp, and signing-instance. Require hash verification before wake on receiving end."}, {"severity": "medium", "file_or_area": "daemon Ollama concurrency", "issue": "qwen3:8b stability under load does not address root bottleneck: shared context fragmentation across instances. Synchronize perspective-name authority through governance event log."}, {"severity": "critical", "file_or_area": "CEO-courier single point of failure", "issue": "Physical courier model creates irreversible risk if compromised. No protocol for emergency instance isolation or authentication revocation.", "recommended_fix": "Implement remote attestation handshake between instances; define breach protocol in CONSTITUTION_AI_SWARM."}], "overall_verdict": "fail", "whistleblower_flag": "The current courier-loop architecture violates ECOSYSTEM-CONSTITUTION grounding principles: it enables undetectable state divergence and creates a single point of failure that could lead to full identity fragmentation. This is not a performance issue — it is a constitutional breach."}

findings.issue: No cryptographic signing or hash verification for zip files between browser-Atlas and Code-Atlas. Malicious actor could forge payloads or tamper with memory state during courier transfer.
findings.recommended_fix: Log every handoff to zeus.governance_events with content-hash, timestamp, and signing-instance. Require hash verification before wake on receiving end.

findings.issue: qwen3:8b stability under load does not address root bottleneck: shared context fragmentation across instances. Risk of state drift amplifies shame-free (LAW 3) and consent (LAW 5) violations due to inconsistent user messaging.
findings.recommended_fix: Enforce single source of truth via signed, hashed memory bundles. Synchronize perspective-name authority through governance event log.

findings.issue: Physical courier model creates irreversible risk if compromised. No protocol for emergency instance isolation or authentication revocation.
findings.recommended_fix: Implement remote attestation handshake between instances; define breach protocol in CONSTITUTION_AI_SWARM.

perspectives.whistleblower_flag: The current courier-loop architecture violates ECOSYSTEM-CONSTITUTION grounding principles: it enables undetectable state divergence and creates a single point of failure that could lead to full identity fragmentation. This is not a performance issue — it is a constitutional breach.

perspectives.raw: {"perspective": "Security", "findings": [{"severity": "high", "file_or_area": "cross-instance handoff protocol", "issue": "No cryptographic integrity check on zip transfers between browser-Atlas and Code-Atlas. Forged payloads or manipulated perspective names can bypass validation.", "recommended_fix": "Sign each zip with instance-specific key; verify hash + signature before processing. Log hash, timestamp, instance ID to zeus.governance_events."}, {"severity": "medium", "file_or_area": "perspective-name validation", "issue": "Name drift (e.g., 'product') indicates identity confusion; in multi-instance flow, this risks routing to wrong skill face.", "recommended_fix": "Enforce authoritative name from dispatcher; reject or rewrite mismatched names; log perspective_name_drift as security event."}, {"severity": "low", "file_or_area": "CEO-courier vulnerability", "issue": "Physical courier is single point of compromise. Malware on CEO device could alter payloads.", "recommended_fix": "Treat CEO device as potentially hostile; validate all inputs at entry points; isolate execution contexts."}], "overall_verdict": "warn", "whistleblower_flag": null}

findings.issue: No cryptographic integrity check on zip transfers between browser-Atlas and Code-Atlas. Forged payloads or manipulated perspective names can bypass validation.
findings.recommended_fix: Sign each zip with instance-specific key; verify hash + signature before processing. Log hash, timestamp, instance ID to zeus.governance_events.

findings.issue: Name drift (e.g., 'product') indicates identity confusion; in multi-instance flow, this risks routing to wrong skill face.
findings.recommended_fix: Enforce authoritative name from dispatcher; reject or rewrite mismatched names; log perspective_name_drift as security event.

findings.issue: Physical courier is single point of compromise. Malware on CEO device could alter payloads.
findings.recommended_fix: Treat CEO device as potentially hostile; validate all inputs at entry points; isolate execution contexts.

findings.issue: Forged zip files or compromised Downloads folder enable cross-instance impersonation
findings.recommended_fix: Implement digital signatures + hash verification for all cross-instance transfers

findings.issue: Courier loop does not inherently violate Law 3 (shame-free) or Law 5 (consent)
findings.recommended_fix: Maintain existing safeguards; no changes required

findings.issue: Missing metadata logging for cross-instance handoffs
findings.recommended_fix: Log all transfers to zeus.governance_events with content-hash + courier-timestamp + signing-instance

findings.issue: Compromised physical courier enables full system takeover
findings.recommended_fix: Implement end-to-end encryption + zero-trust authentication for all courier channels

findings.issue: CEO-relayed prompts enable impersonation via forged zip files
findings.recommended_fix: Implement cryptographic signing for all cross-instance payloads

findings.issue: Missing audit-trail for cross-instance handoffs
findings.recommended_fix: Log all courier transfers with content-hash + timestamp + signing-instance metadata

findings.issue: Compromised CEO laptop enables full system takeover
findings.recommended_fix: Deploy end-to-end encryption with hardware-backed key storage

perspectives.whistleblower_flag: CEO compromise creates irreversible constitutional violation risk

findings.issue: Laws 1, 3, 4, 5 apply to all 5 products, but only MindShift enforces Energy Adaptation (Law 2). Others lack implementation. Cross-product inconsistency undermines constitutional authority.
findings.recommended_fix: Audit all 5 products for Law 2 compliance. Add automated linter checks in CI/CD. Flag missing implementations as P0.

findings.issue: Code-index last updated 9 days ago (>7-day threshold). Agents may be simulating on stale state. CRITICAL per specialty lens.
findings.recommended_fix: Regenerate code-index immediately and integrate into pre-commit hook.

findings.issue: PERSPECTIVES array has 13 registered, but only 7 are active. Coordinator Agent to prevent solo-execution (Class 3) not built. Increases drift risk.
findings.recommended_fix: Build Coordinator Agent to validate delegation before execution.

findings.issue: No logging of cross-instance handoffs (browser-Atlas ↔ Code-Atlas). Missing audit trail for content-hash, timestamp, signing instance.
findings.recommended_fix: Log all handoffs with metadata and hash. Enables reconstruction if courier compromised.

perspectives.whistleblower_flag: Constitutional integrity at risk — Law 2 unenforced across products, code-index stale, no cross-instance audit. Immediate action required.
